title: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights
source: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
commitment: Human beings are inviolable. Every human being is entitled to respect for life and the integrity of the person, and no one may be arbitrarily deprived of this right.

title: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights
source: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
commitment: Every individual has the right to liberty and to the security of the person. No one may be deprived of freedom except for reasons and conditions previously laid down by law.

title: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights
source: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
commitment: The right to property shall be guaranteed and may only be encroached upon in the interest of public need or general interest and in accordance with appropriate laws.

title: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights
source: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
commitment: Every individual shall have the right to enjoy the best attainable state of physical and mental health, and states shall take measures to protect the health of their people.

title: African Charter on Human and Peoples' Rights
source: African Commission on Human and Peoples' Rights
commitment: All peoples shall have the right to a general satisfactory environment favourable to their development.

title: Constitution of the Democratic Republic of Congo
commitment: The human person is sacred. The state has the obligation to respect and protect the person. Every person has the right to life, physical integrity, and free development of personality.

title: Constitution of the Democratic Republic of Congo
commitment: All Congolese have the right to enjoy national wealth. The state has the duty to redistribute it equitably and to guarantee the right to development.

title: Democratic Republic of Congo Mining Code
source: EITI / DRC Mining Code reform materials
articleReference: Community development and local benefit obligations
commitment: Mining governance should require community development commitments, local benefit mechanisms, and transparent reporting of obligations affecting mining communities.

title: Mali Agreement for Peace and Reconciliation
source: UN / Mali peace process public materials
commitment: Peace implementation should strengthen security, reconciliation, local governance, and protection of communities affected by conflict.

title: African Union Convention on Preventing and Combating Corruption
commitment: State parties commit to prevent, detect, punish, and eradicate corruption and related offences in public and private sectors.

title: Sudan Constitutional Declaration
commitment: The constitutional framework recognizes rights and freedoms and requires state authorities to protect people from unlawful violence, detention, and rights violations.

title: Guiding Principles on Internal Displacement
articleReference: Principles on protection from displacement and assistance
commitment: Internally displaced persons should be protected from arbitrary displacement, violence, and deprivation of humanitarian assistance.

title: Mozambique peace and recovery commitments
source: Public peace and recovery policy materials
commitment: Recovery efforts should support community security, reintegration, service restoration, and locally led resilience after conflict.
